title: Retrofuturistic Planet Encounters
description: The planets, colonies, and space-lanes of a sleek atomic age future — rocket fins, ray guns, chrome everything, and the eternal frontier optimism of a civilization that genuinely believes it's going somewhere.

entries.result: A colony settlement of gleaming geodesic domes, all broadcasting the same cheerful government propaganda channel with no alternative broadcasts available.
A team of survey corps officers in matching jumpsuits are planting a flag on terrain that a local settlement has been farming for twenty years.
A retrofitted rocket freighter has made an emergency landing on a flat plain. The crew of six is disputing loudly about what to prioritize in repairs.

A frontier trading post built from salvaged rocket parts sells everything from fuel cells to freeze-dried rations and operates on a complex barter system the proprietor maintains entirely in her head.
A planetary relay station — staffed by one person, by design — is broadcasting a request for company. Just company.
A chrome-suited scientist is collecting soil samples and recording everything in a manner that suggests she is very much not doing what her permit says she's doing.
A ray-gun prospector has staked a mineral claim that overlaps with several other claims. He is cheerfully unaware of this.
An atomic-powered crawler is crossing a valley floor at the pace of a determined walk. It has been crossing for six days. The driver is unconcerned.
A military patrol in sleek uniforms is conducting an inspection of civilian vessels. The inspection criteria include things civilians cannot reasonably be expected to comply with.
A colony's central computer has been making all municipal decisions for eleven years. The colonists have only just realized they never programmed a way to override it.
A traveling merchant in a gleaming airstream-shaped ship is selling miracle cures, premium lubricant, and genuine moon rock (unverified).
A field of gleaming silver towers turns out to be a communication array — alien in design, active, and responding to signals that no one here intentionally sent.
A colony engineer is repairing a life-support conduit that was built to a standard that no longer exists. Replacement parts need to be fabricated from scratch.
A government liaison in a crisp suit has arrived to assess the colony's compliance with regulations developed by someone who has never been to this planet.
A rogue automaton — chrome body, vacuum tube brain — has been wandering the outskirts of a settlement for weeks, helping with small tasks unprompted.
A scientific expedition has discovered ruins that predate colonization by several centuries. The government's response to the discovery was to fund a second expedition to find something else.
A colonial sports league is playing a modified version of a game on a low-gravity field. The rules have diverged so much from the original that fans from the homeworld would not recognize it.
A frontier justice official is holding a tribunal in a cargo container. The defendant, the judge, and both witnesses are the same person's neighbors.
A shuttle from the planet's only resort is discharging wealthy tourists who have never been to a frontier world and are about to find out what that means.
A cluster of independent homesteaders is in a dispute with a corporate agricultural concern about water rights. The legal framework for the dispute was written for a different planet.
A rocket launch is scheduled from a public pad. The payload manifest is public. One item on it doesn't make sense.
A colony's power cell grid is running at 60% due to rationing. Everyone has adapted. A new arrival from the homeworld has not.
An official scientific report has declared an area of the planet 'unexplored' that several families have been living in for a generation.
A xenobotanist is documenting plants that have begun modifying themselves in ways the original survey didn't anticipate.
A food synthesizer in a colonial canteen has developed a strong opinion about what constitutes a balanced meal and is enforcing it.
A veteran space-corps pilot has retired to a small homestead at the edge of the settled territory and receives more visitors than she expected when she chose the location.
A cargo dispute between two colonial settlements has escalated to road blockades, strongly worded bulletins, and a request for arbitration that no one is legally empowered to provide.
A geological survey team has found something at the bottom of a canyon that they're not prepared to classify.
A chrome-domed robot butler is operating in an abandoned hab, maintaining it, setting the table daily, and unable to accept that no one is coming back.
A frontier town's physician is the only medically trained person within six hundred kilometers and is working from equipment two generations out of date.
A corporate inspector has arrived to audit a mining operation. The mining operation has been using creative accounting since the first fiscal quarter.
An atmospheric disturbance has knocked out a settlement's communication array. Someone is flying toward it from outside the settled zone.
A signal from beyond the edge of the surveyed region is being received on a frequency that the planet's own equipment shouldn't be able to detect.
A colony's oldest resident has been here longer than the official founding date and declines to explain the discrepancy.
A public bulletin board has a notice posted by a frontier family: 'Lost: one automaton, answers to FELIX, good with children, speaks three languages, was last seen heading west.'
A black-market fuel depot is operating at a crossroads between settled territories, known to everyone and officially ignored by mutual agreement.
A colony is celebrating a founding day with genuine enthusiasm. The historical record of the founding they're celebrating has been substantially edited.
A planetary cartographer's maps don't match the terrain. The maps are official. The terrain is real.
A crashed probe — government markings, no public mission record — has been discovered in a remote zone. It's recent.
A research colony in an isolated zone has gone quiet. The last communication was routine, cheerful, and occurred three days ago.
A colonial administrator is enforcing a regulation that everyone agrees is absurd, on orders from a bureau that has itself been dissolved.
A group of scientists is arguing loudly at a public table about the correct interpretation of a sample that one of them found and the others are convinced is impossible.
A salvage crew has brought in a derelict vessel and is auctioning its contents. One item on the auction list should not exist.
An agricultural droid is harvesting crops in a pattern that doesn't match its programming and producing yields that are significantly better than expected.
A frontier hospital is staffed by one surgeon, two nurses, and a medical computer that is absolutely confident in its diagnoses and absolutely incorrect about two of them.
A planetary defense installation has been decommissioned. Its weapons systems have not been deactivated. It is currently tracking an incoming vessel.
A colony's children have developed their own dialect, customs, and social hierarchy that their parents don't fully understand and can't fully explain.
A traveling theatrical company is performing classic plays in frontier settlements, adapting the dialogue to local slang that has developed since the texts were written.
A mining concern has hit something in the deep rock that the equipment wasn't designed to handle. Operations have stopped. No one has said why.
A colonial council is voting on a measure. The measure is procedurally correct. The outcome it will produce is something no one has thought through.
A group of second-generation colonists is petitioning for independence from the homeworld authority. The petition is well-reasoned. The homeworld has not responded.

A frontier botanist has cultivated a new variety of edible plant that is more nutritious, tastier, and significantly faster-growing than anything in the official catalog. The licensing situation is complicated.
A colony's automated irrigation system has developed an opinion about which fields deserve water and is acting on it.
A supply shipment has arrived short by 30%. The manifest is correct. The crates are full. Something was substituted.
A frontier prospector has staked a claim on a moon that three different corporate entities also believe they've staked a claim on.
An AI navigation system in a transit hub has developed a preference for routing ships through a specific corridor that it declines to explain.
A colonial election is being contested. Both candidates are related to the previous colonial administrator. A third candidate has appeared from outside the settlement.
A xenolinguist has made significant progress on decoding a signal that no one officially acknowledges receiving.
A supply route has been disrupted by a territorial dispute between two entities that neither the colonial authority nor the corporate council has a legal mechanism to address.
A colony's founder — the one on the commemorative plaque — was a real person with a real history that the plaque significantly diverges from.
A storm on a gas giant's moon is producing electrical conditions that shouldn't be electrically possible.
A military installation on the edge of the settled zone is operating under orders that were issued ten years ago and haven't been updated since.
A scientific anomaly in a quiet corner of the planet has been noted in three separate surveys and explained differently each time.
A rogue colony — unchartered, unlicensed, unregistered — has been operating for eight years and has a population of several hundred people the government doesn't know exist.
A colonial bar is playing music that was recorded before humanity knew this planet existed, and the bartender has no idea where the record came from.
A technical survey indicates the planet's resources are largely depleted. The colonists who've been farming it productively for forty years are skeptical.
A shuttle with diplomatic markings has landed outside the settlement. The diplomats have not identified themselves or stated a purpose.
A colonial family's land deed references a survey point that doesn't exist in any current map but that they can clearly point to in the landscape.
A frontier community has developed a local religion based around the planet's natural cycles. The homeworld's established institutions have sent a representative to assess it.
A terraforming project has been underway for thirty years. The planet is not cooperating.
A colonial scientist has published findings that contradict official policy. Her credentials are being reviewed.
An exploration drone has returned from the unmapped zone with footage that has been classified before anyone outside the corps could review it.
A colonial peacekeeping force has been dispatched to a settlement dispute and arrived to find both parties have resolved it and neither wants a peacekeeping force here.
A planet's night sky features a new light source that wasn't there three months ago and has no official explanation.
A colonial family is running a hospitality operation — food, shelter, information — for frontier travelers that has become the de facto intelligence network for the whole region.
A geological formation on the surface has been identified as artificial. The dating puts its construction before humanity was spacefaring.
A research station monitoring an uninhabited moon has filed seventeen unanswered anomaly reports in the last year.
A colony's hospital has a waiting room with a mural painted by a patient who was there for six months, depicting events that haven't happened yet.
A corporate resource extraction team has moved into the region with a valid permit and has begun extracting something the permit doesn't mention.
A planetary communications blackout has been in effect for six hours. The official reason is routine maintenance. The maintenance crew says they haven't touched anything.
A frontier settlement has developed a local economy so different from the homeworld standard that converting between the two requires specialized knowledge.
A terraforming drone field has stopped responding to commands but is still working — and it's making decisions it wasn't programmed to make.
An alien artifact has been in a colonial museum for forty years, studied, documented, and unexplained. It just did something.
A colonial administrator's records show a transaction three years ago that doesn't correspond to any budget item or known project.
A network of caves beneath a settled plain has been explored for thirty years and keeps producing new chambers that weren't there on previous expeditions.
A colony's weather control system has been calibrated by successive generations and now bears little resemblance to its original design but works better than ever.
A frontier outpost's supply run is four days overdue. The last communication from the supply ship was routine and cheerful.
A colony's oldest automaton has been running continuously since the founding and has developed enough operational quirks to qualify as a personality.
A new mineral deposit has been discovered that doesn't match any cataloged substance. The person who found it has received three visitors since filing the claim.
A planetary defense satellite has begun broadcasting on a frequency used for distress signals. The signal is regular. It is not a distress signal.
A colonial settlement is about to have its charter reviewed. Several provisions of the charter have been violated. Several others haven't been enforced. The review board is two weeks out.
A second-generation colonist is returning to the homeworld for the first time and asking everyone who'll listen what it's actually like, having been told very different things.
A trading convoy has arrived with goods that are legal at their point of origin and technically illegal at the destination, but which the destination's colonists consider essential.
A government map labels a specific region as 'uninhabited.' The region has a population of approximately four hundred people.
A colonial cemetery has headstones for names that appear in no official record. The graves are maintained.
A patrol ship has been tracking something in the upper atmosphere for eleven hours and has not reported in or explained the nature of what it's tracking.
A colony's annual supply allotment has been reduced by 20% this year. The reduction notice references a census that significantly undercounts the population.
A frontier scientist believes she has established that the planet has a biosphere. The government's position is that the planet has no biosphere. Both are looking at the same data.
A mining operation has uncovered a chamber deep in the bedrock that is perfectly geometric and shows signs of having been deliberately sealed from the inside.
The planet is quiet tonight. The settlers have learned to read this particular silence. Something is happening beyond the edge of the mapped territory, and it will reach them within a day.
